text.title: Earned Value Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Earned Value Management. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: PV (Planned Value) curve and AC (Actual Cost) collection MUST start from frozen scope, schedule, and cost baselines. Estimates-in-flight cannot serve as baseline.

rationale.source: PMI Practice Standard for EVM (2nd ed.)

rule.rationale: EVM math collapses without a stable PV; comparing EV against a moving PV produces nonsense indices.

rule.statement: Earned Value MUST be computed from objective completion criteria (0/50/100 or %-complete rule defined in EVM plan). Subjective 'feels 80%' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Subjective EV is gameable; objective criteria produce auditable indices.

rule.statement: CPI and SPI MUST be reported each period with thresholds: ≥0.95 green, 0.85-0.95 amber, <0.85 red. Red triggers corrective-action plan.
rule.rationale: Threshold-less reporting hides drift; the rule forces explicit action when indices breach.

rule.statement: Each EAC (Estimate at Completion) value MUST cite its method (BAC/CPI, AC + ETC, optimistic, regression). Mixing methods within one report is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Different EAC methods produce different forecasts; uncited values mislead stakeholders about confidence.

rule.statement: Re-baselining (changing PV mid-project) MUST go through change-control with sponsor approval. Silent re-baselining is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent re-baselining hides cost overrun; the rule ties EVM to the change-control audit trail.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Earned Value Management fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Earned Value Management outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
